structure.title: Purpose
structure.text:
Hotel Rules & Regulations
 
(Policies & Regulations – Hospitality)
 
1. Purpose
 
To ensure a safe, comfortable, and respectful environment for all guests and staff by establishing clear 
expectations for behavior and use of hotel facilities.
 
 
2. Scope
 
These regulations apply to all guests, visitors, and occupants within hotel premises including:
 
Guestrooms
Lobby and public areas
Food & beverage outlets
Parking zones
Recreational facilities (pool, gym, spa)
 
 
3. Check-In & Check-Out
 
Minimum Check-in Age: 18 years (valid government ID required)
Guests must register with accurate personal details
Check-out by 11:00 AM (late check-out subject to availability & charges)
Hotel reserves the right to refuse service for safety, fraud, or misconduct concerns
structure.summary: The partial document outlines the rules and regulations of a hotel, focusing on policies to ensure a safe and respectful environment for guests and staff. It specifies the scope of the regulations, covering all areas of the hotel premises, including guestrooms, public areas, dining outlets, parking, and recreational facilities. It also details check-in and check-out policies, including the minimum check-in age, ID requirements, accurate registration, check-out timing, and conditions under which service may be refused.

structure.title: Check-In & Check-Out
structure.text:
Hotel Rules & Regulations
 
(Policies & Regulations – Hospitality)
 
1. Purpose
 
To ensure a safe, comfortable, and respectful environment for all guests and staff by establishing clear 
expectations for behavior and use of hotel facilities.
 
 
2. Scope
 
These regulations apply to all guests, visitors, and occupants within hotel premises including:
 
Guestrooms
Lobby and public areas
Food & beverage outlets
Parking zones
Recreational facilities (pool, gym, spa)
 
 
3. Check-In & Check-Out
 
Minimum Check-in Age: 18 years (valid government ID required)
Guests must register with accurate personal details
Check-out by 11:00 AM (late check-out subject to availability & charges)
Hotel reserves the right to refuse service for safety, fraud, or misconduct concerns 
Keys/cards remain hotel property and must be returned upon check-out.
 
 
4. Payment & Security Deposit
 
Full payment or authorized guarantee required at check-in
A security deposit may be collected to cover incidentals or damages
Outstanding balances must be settled before departure
 
Fraudulent payment or charge-back will be handled under local laws.
 
 
5. Safety & Security Rules
 
 
No weapons, explosives, hazardous materials allowed
 
 
Emergency exits and corridors must not be obstructed
 
 
Do not tamper with:
 
Smoke detectors
Fire alarms
Sprinkler systems
structure.summary: The partial document outlines the rules and regulations for a hotel, focusing on creating a safe and respectful environment for guests and staff. It covers the purpose and scope of the regulations, including areas such as guestrooms, public spaces, and recreational facilities. Key policies include check-in and check-out procedures, payment and security deposit requirements, and safety and security rules. Specific details include minimum check-in age, ID requirements, payment obligations, prohibition of hazardous materials, and guidelines for emergency exits and safety equipment.

structure.summary: The partial document outlines hotel policies regarding keys/cards, payment, security deposits, and safety rules. It specifies that keys/cards are hotel property and must be returned at check-out. Full payment or an authorized guarantee is required at check-in, and a security deposit may be collected for incidentals or damages. Outstanding balances must be cleared before departure, and fraudulent payments will be addressed under local laws. Safety rules prohibit weapons, explosives, and hazardous materials, require emergency exits and corridors to remain unobstructed, and forbid tampering with smoke detectors, fire alarms, and sprinkler systems.

structure.title: Safety & Security Rules
structure.text:
Keys/cards remain hotel property and must be returned upon check-out.
 
 
4. Payment & Security Deposit
 
Full payment or authorized guarantee required at check-in
A security deposit may be collected to cover incidentals or damages
Outstanding balances must be settled before departure
 
Fraudulent payment or charge-back will be handled under local laws.
 
 
5. Safety & Security Rules
 
 
No weapons, explosives, hazardous materials allowed
 
 
Emergency exits and corridors must not be obstructed
 
 
Do not tamper with:
 
Smoke detectors
Fire alarms
Sprinkler systems 
 
Report safety hazards or suspicious activity to staff immediately
 
 
Hotel is monitored by CCTV in public areas for safety.
 
 
6. Noise & Conduct
 
Quiet hours: 22:00 – 07:00
No parties, loud music, or disruptive behavior in rooms/hallways
Guests must treat staff and other guests with respect
Harassment, aggression, discrimination, or illegal behavior leads to immediate eviction
 
Hotel reserves the right to remove any individual causing disturbance without refund.
 
 
7. Visitor Policy
 
Visitors must register at front desk and comply with hotel rules
Visitors allowed only until 22:00 unless registered as an additional guest
Occupancy of guests must not exceed room limits
 
Hotel will not permit unregistered persons beyond approved hours.
structure.summary: The partial document outlines hotel policies regarding key/card returns, payment and security deposits, safety and security rules, noise and conduct, and visitor policies. It specifies that keys/cards are hotel property, full payment and security deposits may be required, and outstanding balances must be cleared before departure. Safety rules prohibit weapons, hazardous materials, and tampering with safety equipment, with CCTV monitoring in public areas. Noise and conduct rules enforce quiet hours, prohibit disruptive behavior, and mandate respectful treatment of staff and guests, with eviction for violations. Visitor policies require registration, limit visiting hours, and enforce room occupancy limits.

structure.summary: The partial document outlines hotel policies on safety, noise, conduct, and visitor regulations. It emphasizes reporting safety hazards, the use of CCTV in public areas, and maintaining quiet hours from 22:00 to 07:00. It prohibits parties, loud music, disruptive behavior, harassment, aggression, discrimination, and illegal activities, with violations leading to eviction without refund. Visitors must register at the front desk, comply with hotel rules, and are only allowed until 22:00 unless registered as additional guests, with occupancy limits strictly enforced.

structure.title: Visitor Policy
structure.text:
Report safety hazards or suspicious activity to staff immediately
 
 
Hotel is monitored by CCTV in public areas for safety.
 
 
6. Noise & Conduct
 
Quiet hours: 22:00 – 07:00
No parties, loud music, or disruptive behavior in rooms/hallways
Guests must treat staff and other guests with respect
Harassment, aggression, discrimination, or illegal behavior leads to immediate eviction
 
Hotel reserves the right to remove any individual causing disturbance without refund.
 
 
7. Visitor Policy
 
Visitors must register at front desk and comply with hotel rules
Visitors allowed only until 22:00 unless registered as an additional guest
Occupancy of guests must not exceed room limits
 
Hotel will not permit unregistered persons beyond approved hours.
 
 8. Smoking, Alcohol & Drugs
 
100% Smoke-free Hotel (including vapes & e-cigarettes)
A cleaning fee will be applied if smoking occurs in room
Alcohol consumption allowed only in designated areas
Illegal drugs strictly prohibited — will be reported to authorities
 
 
9. Pets
 
 
Pets allowed only if hotel is pet-friendly and with prior approval
 
 
Guests responsible for:
 
Noise, cleanliness, safety of pet
Damage repair & special cleaning fees
 
 
Service animals permitted as per local law.
 
 
10. Care of Property
 
 
Guests are responsible for any damage to:
structure.summary:
The partial document outlines hotel policies and guidelines, including:

1. Reporting safety hazards or suspicious activity and the use of CCTV for safety.
2. Noise and conduct rules, including quiet hours, prohibition of disruptive behavior, and consequences for harassment or illegal actions.
3. Visitor policy, requiring visitor registration, adherence to rules, and restrictions on unregistered persons.
4. Smoking, alcohol, and drug policies, including a smoke-free environment, designated alcohol consumption areas, and prohibition of illegal drugs.
5. Pet policies, allowing pets only with prior approval, guest responsibility for pet-related issues, and allowance of service animals as per local law.
6. Guest responsibility for any property damage.

It specifies a 100% smoke-free environment, restrictions on alcohol consumption, and a prohibition on illegal drugs. Pet policies include prior approval for pet-friendly hotels, guest responsibility for pet-related issues, and allowance of service animals as per local law. structure.title: Care of Property
structure.text:
8. Smoking, Alcohol & Drugs
 
100% Smoke-free Hotel (including vapes & e-cigarettes)
A cleaning fee will be applied if smoking occurs in room
Alcohol consumption allowed only in designated areas
Illegal drugs strictly prohibited — will be reported to authorities
 
 
9. Pets
 
 
Pets allowed only if hotel is pet-friendly and with prior approval
 
 
Guests responsible for:
 
Noise, cleanliness, safety of pet
Damage repair & special cleaning fees
 
 
Service animals permitted as per local law.
 
 
10. Care of Property
 
 
Guests are responsible for any damage to: 
Room furniture & linens
Electronics & fixtures
Hotel property or facilities
 
 
Missing/damaged items will be charged at replacement cost
 
 
Do not move furniture without staff approval.
 
 
11. Housekeeping & Room Maintenance
 
Standard service once daily unless "Do Not Disturb" sign is displayed
Rooms with continuous DND for 24 hours may require mandatory safety check
Towels & linens replaced based on sustainability guidelines
 
Any maintenance issues must be reported immediately.
 
 
12. Fire & Emergency Procedures
 
In case of fire: Nearest exit � Assemble at meeting point
Do not use elevators during evacuation
Follow directions from hotel staff/emergency responders
Emergency exit maps are placed behind guestroom doors
structure.summary:
The partial document outlines hotel policies and guidelines on the following topics:

1. **Smoking, Alcohol & Drugs**: The hotel is 100% smoke-free, including vapes and e-cigarettes, with cleaning fees for violations. Alcohol is allowed only in designated areas, and illegal drugs are strictly prohibited and reported to authorities.

2. **Pets**: Pets are allowed only in pet-friendly hotels with prior approval. Guests are responsible for pet-related noise, cleanliness, safety, and any damage or cleaning fees. Service animals are permitted as per local laws.

3. **Care of Property**: Guests are accountable for any damage to room furniture, linens, electronics, fixtures, or hotel property. Missing or damaged items will be charged at replacement cost, and furniture should not be moved without staff approval.

4. **Housekeeping & Room Maintenance**: Standard housekeeping is provided daily unless a "Do Not Disturb" sign is displayed. Rooms with continuous DND for 24 hours may undergo mandatory safety checks. Towels and linens are replaced based on sustainability guidelines, and maintenance issues must be reported immediately.

5. **Fire & Emergency Procedures**: In case of fire, guests should use the nearest exit, assemble at the meeting point, avoid elevators, and follow staff or emergency responder instructions. Emergency exit maps are provided behind guestroom doors.

 False alarms or misuse of safety equipment will incur penalties.
 
 
13. Pool, Gym & Recreational Facilities
 
Operating hours and safety instructions must be followed
Proper attire required
Children under 12 must be accompanied by an adult
No glassware or food near pools
 
Use of facilities is at guest’s own risk.
 
 
14. Lost & Found
 
Hotel is not liable for valuables not stored in in-room safe
Unclaimed lost items are kept as per legal guidelines
Verified owners must present ID to claim items
 
 
15. Parking
 
 
Parking privileges are subject to availability

15. Parking
 
 
Parking privileges are subject to availability
 
 Hotel not responsible for:
 
Theft
Damage
Lost personal items inside vehicles
 
 
Follow parking signage and speed limits inside hotel premises.
 
 
16. Food & Beverage
 
Outside food delivery must not disturb other guests
Cooking inside rooms (stoves, hot plates) not permitted
No storing of strong-odor foods that may cause discomfort or damage
 
 
17. Photography & Recording
 
Taking photos/videos of other guests without consent is prohibited
Professional shoots require hotel’s prior approval
Drones not allowed on property without authorization
 
 
18. Legal Compliance
 
Guests must comply with all:

4. Parking regulations, including availability, liability disclaimers, and adherence to signage and speed limits.
5. Food and beverage restrictions, such as prohibitions on cooking in rooms and storing strong-odor foods.
6. Photography and recording rules, including consent requirements, restrictions on professional shoots, and drone usage.
7. Legal compliance obligations for guests.

structure.title: Legal Compliance
structure.text:
Hotel not responsible for:
 
Theft
Damage
Lost personal items inside vehicles
 
 
Follow parking signage and speed limits inside hotel premises.
 
 
16. Food & Beverage
 
Outside food delivery must not disturb other guests
Cooking inside rooms (stoves, hot plates) not permitted
No storing of strong-odor foods that may cause discomfort or damage
 
 
17. Photography & Recording
 
Taking photos/videos of other guests without consent is prohibited
Professional shoots require hotel’s prior approval
Drones not allowed on property without authorization
 
 
18. Legal Compliance
 
Guests must comply with all: 
Local laws
Immigration regulations
Health & safety standards
 
Hotel may cooperate with law enforcement when required.
 
 
19. Violations & Penalties
 
The hotel reserves the right to:
 
Terminate the stay of any violator without refund
Charge for damages or extra cleaning
Ban re-entry or future bookings
 
Misconduct may also result in legal action.
 
 
Acknowledgment
 
Staying at the hotel implies that the guest has read, understood, and agreed to comply with these rules and 
regulations.
 
 
End of Document
structure.summary:
The partial document outlines the hotel's policies and rules for guests, covering several key areas:  

1. **Liability**: The hotel is not responsible for theft, damage, or lost personal items inside vehicles.  
2. **Parking**: Guests must follow parking signage and speed limits on the premises.  
3. **Food & Beverage**: Restrictions include no cooking inside rooms, no storing strong-odor foods, and ensuring outside food delivery does not disturb others.  
4. **Photography & Recording**: Prohibits taking photos/videos of other guests without consent, requires approval for professional shoots, and bans unauthorized drone use.  
5. **Legal Compliance**: Guests must adhere to local laws, immigration regulations, and health and safety standards, with the hotel cooperating with law enforcement when necessary.  
6. **Violations & Penalties**: The hotel reserves the right to terminate stays, charge for damages, ban future bookings, and take legal action for misconduct.  
7. **Acknowledgment**: Staying at the hotel implies agreement to these rules and regulations.
